mappings.reason: Designing and maintaining a secure network architecture with segmentation and least privilege directly prevents unauthorized parties from accessing network segments containing routing information.
Securely managing network infrastructure using secure protocols (SSH/HTTPS) prevents disclosure of routing configurations and private IP data during administrative access.
Encrypting sensitive data in transit protects private IP addresses and routing information from interception and disclosure to unauthorized parties on the network.
Securely managing enterprise assets via secure administrative protocols prevents disclosure of configuration data, including routing information, across all asset types.
Centralizing network AAA controls access to network infrastructure, ensuring only authorized parties can access devices holding routing information.
Adopting secure network management and communication protocols (e.g., 802.1X, WPA2) prevents unauthorized network access and sniffing of routing data.
Traffic filtering between network segments enforces the segmentation architecture that restricts unauthorized parties from reaching routing information domains.
